name: 4-State 3-Filter TQQQ
description: A four-state trend follower on QQQ that sizes TQQQ exposure proportional to how many of three bullish filters are active. The three filters are an Aroon oscillator, a Donchian-200 midline price test, and a rate-of-change momentum check. All four TQQQ/BIL allocations (100/0, 70/30, 30/70, 0/100) are tied to the filter count.
overfit_notes: Three independently tuned filters: Aroon-25 with AroonUp>70 threshold, Donchian-200 midline, and ROC(60)>0. The AroonUp>70 threshold and the specific step-down weights (100/70/30/0%) are mildly tuned.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three binary filters: (1) AroonUp(25)>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; (2) QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline; (3) ROC(60)>0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ/30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ/70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on each filter-count change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State Aroon-25 + Donchian-150
description: A three-state trend follower that pairs Aroon-25 with a shorter Donchian-150 midline instead of the more common 200-day window. This makes the channel test somewhat more reactive to intermediate-term price changes. The three states map to 100% TQQQ, 50/50 TQQQ/BIL, and 100% BIL.
overfit_notes: Standard Aroon-25 indicator. The Donchian window is 150 rather than the canonical 200, a mild tuning of one parameter.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Aroon-25: AroonUp>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; Donchian-150 midline: QQQ price > midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 50% TQQQ/50% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 4-State Aroon + D100 + D200
description: A four-state trend follower that counts how many of three independent regime filters are bullish: Aroon-25 momentum, a Donchian-100 midline, and a Donchian-200 midline. The TQQQ allocation steps from 100% down to 70%, 30%, and 0% as fewer filters confirm the uptrend.
overfit_notes: Three filters with mildly tuned parameters: Aroon-25 with AroonUp>70 threshold, Donchian-100 and Donchian-200 midlines. Using two Donchian channels of different lengths is a non-canonical design choice.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) AroonUp(25)>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; (2) QQQ > Donchian-100 midline; (3) QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ/30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ/70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on filter-count change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State 60/40 Middle
description: A three-state trend follower on QQQ using Aroon-25 and Donchian-200 midline filters. The middle mixed state holds a more conservative 60/40 TQQQ/BIL split rather than 50/50. Full bull means 100% TQQQ and full bear means 100% BIL.
overfit_notes: Two standard indicators with a slightly varied middle allocation: 60/40 instead of 50/50. Otherwise uses canonical Aroon-25 and Donchian-200.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Aroon-25: AroonUp>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; Donchian-200 midline: QQQ price > midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 60% TQQQ/40% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 4-State TQQQ/Top-1 Step-Down
description: A four-state rotator that uses Aroon-25 and two Donchian channels to count bullish signals and step down from TQQQ into the largest mega-cap stock and finally cash. At full bull it holds 100% TQQQ; at two signals it splits evenly with Top-1; at one signal it goes fully into Top-1; at zero it splits Top-1 and cash.
overfit_notes: Three regime filters: Aroon-25, Donchian-100, and Donchian-200 midlines. The particular step-down allocation schedule (100/50-50/100-Top1/50-50-BIL) is non-standard and involves several tuned design choices.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) AroonUp(25)>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; (2) QQQ > Donchian-100 midline; (3) QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on filter-count change

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top-100 by dollar volume → top-1 by market cap

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-1 mega-cap / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State Aroon-10 + Donchian-200
description: A three-state trend follower that swaps the standard Aroon-25 for a faster Aroon-10 paired with the canonical Donchian-200 midline. The shorter Aroon period makes the momentum signal more reactive to recent price extremes. Bull, mixed, and bear states map to 100% TQQQ, 50/50, and 100% BIL.
overfit_notes: Aroon period shortened from the typical 25 to 10 — a mild tuning. Donchian-200 is standard. AroonUp>70 threshold is conventional.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Aroon-10: AroonUp>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; Donchian-200 midline: QQQ price > midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 50% TQQQ/50% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State ROC(20) + Donchian-200
description: A three-state trend follower that replaces the Aroon oscillator with a 20-day rate-of-change indicator on QQQ alongside the standard Donchian-200 midline. When both momentum and the channel position are bullish it holds TQQQ fully; partial confirmation yields a 50/50 blend; both bearish yields full cash.
overfit_notes: ROC(20)>0 is a canonical momentum threshold. Donchian-200 midline is standard. Two-indicator, textbook-level design.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 50% TQQQ/50% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

overfit_notes: ROC period of 10 is shorter than the more typical 20 or 60, a mild tuning. Donchian-200 midline is standard. Both signals at canonical zero threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(10)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

overfit_notes: ROC period of 40 is a non-standard intermediate value between 20 and 60. Donchian-200 is standard. Both thresholds are canonical (zero crossing).

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(40)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry

description: A four-state trend follower that counts three bullish conditions — positive 20-day momentum, price above a Donchian-100 midline, and price above a Donchian-200 midline — to step TQQQ exposure from 100% down to 0% as conditions deteriorate. This replaces the Aroon oscillator from earlier variants with ROC.
overfit_notes: Three filters with standard zero-crossing thresholds for ROC. Using both Donchian-100 and Donchian-200 simultaneously is a non-canonical multi-channel design. Step-down weights (100/70/30/0%) are fixed.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) ROC(20)>0; (2) QQQ > Donchian-100 midline; (3) QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ/30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ/70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Trend gate

name: 3-State ROC(20)+D200 + Top-1 Defense
description: A three-state rotator that uses ROC(20) and Donchian-200 on QQQ to switch between TQQQ and the largest mega-cap stock as defensive holding. When both signals are bullish it goes fully into TQQQ; in mixed regime it splits evenly with Top-1; in bear regime it holds 100% Top-1 with no cash.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 midline. The choice of a single mega-cap as defense and equal-split in mixed state are design choices; universe selects from top-100 by dollar volume then top-1 by market cap.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 50% TQQQ/50% Top-1 mega-cap; both bear → 100% Top-1

rules.label: Exit

overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200. Middle allocation set to 80/20 — more aggressive than the 50/50 baseline, a mild single-parameter tuning.

rules.label: Trend gate

It eliminates BIL from the partial-bull states and replaces it with a liquid mega-cap equity position.
overfit_notes: Three filters with standard thresholds. The allocation schedule (100% TQQQ / 50-50 TQQQ-Top1 / 100% Top1 / 50-50 Top1-BIL) involves multiple tuned design choices across four states.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) ROC(20)>0; (2) QQQ > Donchian-100 midline; (3) QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Exit

overfit_notes: ROC(60)>0 and Donchian-200 midline are both standard, textbook-level parameters. Two standard components at canonical thresholds.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(60)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry

description: A four-state trend follower that combines three distinct signal types: rate-of-change momentum, Aroon directional strength, and a Donchian-200 price-channel position. The TQQQ allocation steps down proportionally to the number of bullish confirmations, from 100% to 0% in four increments.
overfit_notes: Three diverse but standard indicators: ROC(20)>0, Aroon-25 with AroonUp>70, and Donchian-200. Using all three together is a non-canonical multi-indicator design. Step-down weights are fixed at 100/70/30/0%.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) ROC(20)>0; (2) AroonUp(25)>70 AND AroonUp>AroonDown; (3) QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry

name: 3-State ROC(20)+D200 60/40
description: A three-state trend follower using ROC(20) and Donchian-200 midline on QQQ with a moderate 60/40 TQQQ/BIL split in the mixed state. This is a conservative middle-ground variant between the 50/50 and 70/30 versions.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 midline. Middle allocation is 60/40 — a mild tuning between the most common 50/50 baseline and more aggressive variants.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry

name: 3-State Momentum Accel ROC(20)
description: A three-state trend follower that requires momentum to be both positive and accelerating for full bull exposure. It uses ROC(20) with a 10-day look-back to detect acceleration, combined with a Donchian-200 midline. The full bull state requires all three conditions; partial exposure if one or two are met.
overfit_notes: Three tuned parameters: ROC period of 20, acceleration look-back of 10 days, and Donchian-200. The acceleration condition (current ROC > ROC from 10 days ago) is a non-standard, mildly tuned design.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; ROC(20) > ROC(20) from 10 days ago (momentum accelerating); QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: All three bull → 100% TQQQ; one or two bull (partial) → 50% TQQQ/50% BIL; none → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State ROC+D200 + VIX>35 Panic
description: A three-state trend follower based on ROC(20) and Donchian-200 that adds a VIX panic override: whenever VIX exceeds a high threshold, the strategy forces 100% cash regardless of the trend signals. This creates four effective states but preserves the simple three-state logic when VIX is calm.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 midline. The VIX panic threshold of 35 is a round number but higher than the conventional 30 used in simpler VIX filters — a mild single-parameter addition.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: VIX>35 → 100% BIL (panic override); otherwise: both bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 50% TQQQ/50% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ, CBOE VIX. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: Top-5 60% + 40% TQQQ/BIL Overlay
description: A hybrid rotator that permanently holds 60% in the top-5 mega-cap stocks while switching a 40% overlay between TQQQ (bull) and BIL (bear) based on whether QQQ is above its Donchian-200 midline. This is the 60/40 analog to the 70/30 TQQQ overlay variant.
overfit_notes: Standard Donchian-200 midline gate. The 60/40 base-to-overlay split is a design choice. Universe: top-100 by dollar volume → top-5 by market cap.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > Donchian-200 midline → 40% TQQQ overlay; otherwise 40% BIL

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Always 60% top-5 mega-caps (equal weight). Bull: 40% TQQQ. Bear: 40% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Swap overlay between TQQQ and BIL on regime change

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top-100 by dollar volume → top-5 by market cap

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: top-5 mega-cap stocks / TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State Momentum-Accel + Top-1 Defense
description: A three-state rotator that pairs a momentum-acceleration filter with a Donchian-200 trend gate, and uses the largest mega-cap stock as the defensive holding instead of cash. Full bull (all three conditions) gives 100% TQQQ; mixed gives 50/50 with Top-1; bear gives 100% Top-1.
overfit_notes: Three interacting parameters: ROC(20)>0, acceleration check over 10 days, and Donchian-200. The acceleration condition is non-standard, and combining it with a Top-1 defensive equity rotation adds additional design complexity.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0; ROC(20) > ROC(20) from 10 days ago (momentum accelerating); QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: All three bull → 100% TQQQ; one or two bull → 50% TQQQ/50% Top-1 mega-cap; none → 100% Top-1

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top-100 by dollar volume → top-1 by market cap

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-1 mega-cap stock

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 4-State ROC+D100+D300
description: A four-state trend follower that widens the channel timeframe spread by using a 100-day and a 300-day Donchian midline alongside ROC(20). The very long 300-day channel provides a structural trend anchor while the 100-day channel provides an intermediate signal.
overfit_notes: The 300-day Donchian channel is a non-canonical choice (standard is 200). Three filters with mildly tuned channel lengths. Step-down weights are fixed at 100/70/30/0%.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) ROC(20)>0; (2) QQQ > Donchian-100 midline; (3) QQQ > Donchian-300 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ/30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ/70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on filter-count change

rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

name: 5-State ROC+D100+D200+Top1 Fill
description: A four-state rotator with three regime filters (ROC-20, Donchian-100, Donchian-200) that blends TQQQ with the largest mega-cap stock across all exposure levels, never going to cash. As bull signals diminish, it progressively replaces TQQQ with Top-1, maintaining full equity exposure throughout.
overfit_notes: Three standard filters (ROC-20, D-100, D-200) with allocation steps across four states (100/70-30/30-70/0-100) entirely in equity. Using Top-1 mega-cap as perpetual ballast instead of BIL is a design choice that increases equity correlation.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Three filters: (1) ROC(20)>0; (2) QQQ > Donchian-100 midline; (3) QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ/30% Top-1; n=1 → 30% TQQQ/70% Top-1; n=0 → 100% Top-1 (no cash)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on filter-count change

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top-100 by dollar volume → top-1 by market cap

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-1 mega-cap stock

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: ROC+D200 + 7% Trail Exit
description: A trend follower that enters TQQQ when both ROC(20) and Donchian-200 are bullish, but adds a trailing drawdown exit: it closes the position if QQQ falls more than 7% below its 20-day high, even if the longer-term trend signal remains intact. This provides faster downside protection.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 entry conditions. The 7% trailing drawdown from 20-day high is a non-canonical threshold — the specific percentage is tuned.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ AND QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both conditions true AND QQQ within 7% of 20-day high → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Trend signal turns off OR QQQ drops >7% below 20-day high → 100% BIL

rules.label: Stop-loss
rules.text: Trailing: exit if QQQ falls >7% from its 20-day high

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: TQQQ Pyramid (10%/day)
description: A pyramiding trend follower that gradually scales into TQQQ by adding 10% exposure per day while both ROC(20) and Donchian-200 remain bullish, reaching 100% after ten consecutive bull days. On the first bear signal it exits entirely to cash.
overfit_notes: Key tuned parameter: the 10% daily increment rate (requiring 10 consecutive bull days for full exposure). Also uses standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200. The pyramid step rate is non-canonical.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ AND QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Bull: +10% TQQQ per day (up to 100%); remainder in BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Bear signal → TQQQ to 0%, 100% BIL immediately

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State Dual-ROC + D200
description: A three-state trend follower that requires consensus from both short-term ROC(20) and medium-term ROC(60) for the momentum leg, paired with a Donchian-200 midline. The dual-ROC requirement means both timeframes must agree for the strategy to treat momentum as bullish, reducing false positives.
overfit_notes: ROC(20)>0 AND ROC(60)>0 as a consensus gate are both standard zero-crossing tests. Donchian-200 midline is standard. Two standard components with a straightforward AND logic.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 AND ROC(60)>0 on QQQ (dual-timeframe consensus); QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both filters bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 50% TQQQ/50% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: ROC+D200 + 5% Trail Exit
description: A trend follower identical in structure to the 7%-trail variant but using a tighter 5% trailing drawdown from the 20-day high as the exit trigger. The tighter stop provides faster downside protection at the cost of more frequent exits during normal pullbacks.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 entry. The 5% trailing drawdown threshold from 20-day high is a tighter, non-canonical tuned value.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ AND QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both conditions true AND QQQ within 5% of 20-day high → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Trend signal off OR QQQ drops >5% below 20-day high → 100% BIL

rules.label: Stop-loss
rules.text: Trailing: exit if QQQ falls >5% from its 20-day high

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: ROC+D200 + 10% Trail Exit
description: A trend follower using the same ROC(20) and Donchian-200 entry logic but with a wider 10% trailing drawdown exit from the 20-day high. The looser stop tolerates larger short-term pullbacks before exiting, reducing whipsaw compared to the 5% or 7% variants.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 entry. The 10% trailing drawdown threshold from 20-day high is a round number but non-canonical tuned exit parameter.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ AND QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both conditions true AND QQQ within 10% of 20-day high → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Trend signal off OR QQQ drops >10% below 20-day high → 100% BIL

rules.label: Stop-loss
rules.text: Trailing: exit if QQQ falls >10% from its 20-day high

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State Dual-ROC+D200 70/30
description: A three-state trend follower that requires both ROC(20) and ROC(60) to agree for the momentum signal, combined with a Donchian-200 midline. The mixed state uses a 70/30 TQQQ/BIL allocation for more aggressive partial exposure than the 50/50 baseline.
overfit_notes: Standard dual-ROC consensus (20 and 60) and Donchian-200. The 70/30 mixed-state allocation is a mild single-parameter tuning versus the 50/50 default.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 AND ROC(60)>0 on QQQ; QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both filters bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 70% TQQQ/30% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: TQQQ Pyramid 20%/day
description: A pyramiding trend follower that scales into TQQQ at 20% per day while the trend is confirmed, reaching full exposure in five consecutive bull days. On any bear signal it exits entirely to cash. This is a faster-building variant of the 10%-per-day pyramid.
overfit_notes: The 20% daily step rate (5 days to full exposure) is non-canonical and slightly tuned. Entry conditions (ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200) are standard.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ AND QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Bull: +20% TQQQ per day (up to 100%); remainder in BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Bear signal → TQQQ to 0%, 100% BIL immediately

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: 3-State Triple-ROC+D200 70/30
description: A three-state trend follower that uses a triple-ROC consensus (at least 2 of ROC-10, ROC-30, ROC-60 positive) alongside a Donchian-200 midline, with a 70/30 mixed allocation. The majority-vote approach to ROC reduces reliance on any single timeframe.
overfit_notes: Triple ROC with majority-vote (≥2 of 3) is a non-standard design. The three ROC periods (10, 30, 60) are non-canonical. The 70/30 mixed allocation is a mild tuning. Four combined design choices.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: Majority of ROC(10), ROC(30), ROC(60) positive (≥2 of 3); QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Both filters bull → 100% TQQQ; one bull → 70% TQQQ/30% BIL; both bear → 100% BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Rebalance on state change

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: ROC+D200 + 5% Trail + Top-1 Defense
description: A trend-following rotator that enters TQQQ when ROC(20), Donchian-200, and the trailing drawdown filter all align, and rotates fully into the largest mega-cap stock when any condition fails. This eliminates cash by always maintaining equity exposure through Top-1 as the defensive position.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 entry with a 5% trailing drawdown from 20-day high. Top-1 as defensive equity (no BIL) is a design choice. Four interacting parameters.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; QQQ > Donchian-200 midline; QQQ within 5% of 20-day high

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: All three conditions met → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Any condition fails → 100% Top-1 mega-cap (no cash)

rules.label: Stop-loss
rules.text: Trailing: exit if QQQ falls >5% from its 20-day high

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top-100 by dollar volume → top-1 by market cap

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-1 mega-cap stock

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: TQQQ Pyramid 30%/day
description: The fastest pyramiding variant, adding 30% TQQQ exposure per day while trend conditions hold, reaching full exposure in roughly four consecutive bull days. A single bear day triggers a complete exit to cash. The same ROC(20) and Donchian-200 trend conditions govern entry and exit.
overfit_notes: The 30% daily step rate (approximately 4 days to full exposure) is non-canonical. Entry conditions (ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200) are standard. One non-canonical tuned parameter on top of standard signals.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ AND QQQ > Donchian-200 midline

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Bull: +30% TQQQ per day (up to 100%); remainder in BIL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Bear signal → TQQQ to 0%, 100% BIL immediately

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: ROC+D200 + Trail + Top-1/BIL Fill
description: A trend-following rotator that enters TQQQ when ROC(20), Donchian-200, and a 5% trailing drawdown filter align, but upon exit splits capital between the top mega-cap stock (50%) and cash (50%). This hedges the defensive sleeve by holding both equity and cash during risk-off periods.
overfit_notes: Standard ROC(20)>0 and Donchian-200 entry. The 5% trailing drawdown exit and the 50/50 Top-1/BIL defensive split are tuned design choices. Four interacting parameters across entry and defensive allocation.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: ROC(20)>0 on QQQ; QQQ > Donchian-200 midline; QQQ within 5% of 20-day high

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: All three conditions met → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Any condition fails → 50% Top-1 mega-cap / 50% BIL

rules.label: Stop-loss
rules.text: Trailing: exit if QQQ falls >5% from its 20-day high

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top-100 by dollar volume → top-1 by market cap

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-1 mega-cap stock / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open
